# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch snapshot configuration for automated backups. Configure snapshot repository to S3 or file share for the operations team. Implement snapshot creation and restoration functionality.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for datastore security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: datastore_security

# Elasticsearch Snapshot Configuration for Automated Backups 
elasticsearch_snapshots:
    snapshotRepositoryType: s3   # S3 or file share repository type can be changed as per requirement, default is 'file' if not provided.
    
    repositories:                 
      - name: my-repo             # Repository Name (Required) 
        location: "s3://mybucket/path"       # Location of the S3 bucket where snapshots will be stored in format s3:/your_bucket, file:/local_directory. Required if repositoryType is 'file'  
          type: s3                  # Repository Type (Required)  Can either be "s3", or you can use a local directory for the snapshotRepository to store snapshots locally and then copy them into S3 when needed using an Elasticsearch plugin like `repository-aws`. Default is 'file' if not provided
          s3:                        # Configuration specific to repository type (Required only with "s3")  Can either be a bucket name, or the endpoint of your AWS/Azure storage service where snapshots will reside in format aws:/mybucket-name , azure://mystorageaccount.blob.core.windows.net/. Required if repositoryType is 'aws' /azure
            accessKey: "access_key"          # Access Key for the S3 bucket (Required only with AWS/Azure)  Can either be an IAM role ARN, or a secret key ID in case of using KMS. Defaults to use default credentials if not provided  
              secretKey: 'secret_key'         # Secret access key used by Elasticsearch for accessing the S3 bucket (Required only with AWS/Azure)  Can either be an IAM role ARN, or a secret key ID in case of using KMS. Defaults to use default credentials if not provided  
                region: "us-west"              # Region where your buckets are located(required for S3 repository type). eu-central1 is the only supported AWS/Azure location right now, can be changed as per requirement  Can either be a string like 'eu-east', or an integer representing one of Elasticsearch's regions. Default to use default region if not provided  
          file:                       # Configuration specific for repository type (Required when using local directory)    Path where snapshots will reside in format /local_directory/path  Can either be a path on your filesystem, or an S3 bucket name with the same location and access key. Defaults to use default credentials if not provided
            dir: "/mnt/backups"             # Directory for storing snapshot files (Required when using local directory)   Path where snapshots will reside in format /local_directory/. Can either be a path on your filesystem, or an S3 bucket name with the same location and access key. Defaults to use default credentials if not provided
